doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’ve been having this dry cough for about three days now, and I also have a fever, like, it was 38°C. I’m kinda worried because I’ve been feeling achy and a bit fatigued too… but the cough has gotten a little better, I think.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the cough and fever?
patient_turn: I first noticed the cough, um, about three days ago, and the fever sort of came on around the same time. It’s just been, you know, a bit concerning for me.

doctor_turn: Can you tell me where the cough feels the worst?
patient_turn: Uh, it’s mostly just a dry cough, so it doesn’t really feel like it’s, um, localized anywhere specific. It’s just kind of annoying, you know? I guess it feels worse when I try to talk or, um, take deep breaths.

doctor_turn: How would you describe the cough? Is it persistent, or does it come and go?
patient_turn: Uh, I think it’s pretty persistent, but sometimes it kind of fades a bit, you know? It’s just hard to say exactly when it’s worse.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the cough starting?
patient_turn: I noticed it starting about three days ago, um, like I said. It just kind of came out of nowhere, and I’ve been trying to manage it since then.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any changes in your cough since it first started?
patient_turn: Yeah, um, I think it’s slightly improved since it first started, but it’s still there, you know? It’s just not as intense as it was at the beginning, I guess.

doctor_turn: What does the cough feel like when it happens? Is it a tickle, a tightness, or something else?
patient_turn: Well, it feels kind of dry, like there's a tickle in my throat, which is annoying, and I remember last week I had a really scratchy throat too, but that went away. Oh, and my neighbor's dog barks all night, so maybe that’s contributing to the irritation? It’s just really hard to pinpoint sometimes.

doctor_turn: What does the cough feel like when you have it?
patient_turn: Um, it feels…

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any mucus or blood when you cough?
patient_turn: No, um, I haven’t noticed any mucus or blood at all. It’s just been a dry cough, you know? So that’s one thing I’m kinda relieved about, I guess.

doctor_turn: Have you felt any shortness of breath while coughing or at rest?
patient_turn: Um, no, I haven’t felt any shortness of breath, thankfully. It’s just the cough that’s bothering me, but I can breathe okay otherwise.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any chest pain at all?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any chest pain. It’s just the cough and, um, some mild wheezing, but nothing that feels like pain, you know?

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any night sweats while you sleep?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t noticed any night sweats. I mean, I’ve been feeling a bit achy and fatigued, but no sweating at night or anything like that.

doctor_turn: Have you had any sore throat or nasal congestion recently?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any sore throat or nasal congestion. It’s just the cough and, um, the fever, but no other upper respiratory symptoms, you know?

doctor_turn: Have you had any nausea, vomiting, or abdominal pain lately?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any nausea, vomiting, or abdominal pain at all. It’s just been the cough and the fever, really. I’m glad it’s not anything more serious, I guess.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any changes in your urination, like pain or increased frequency?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t noticed any changes in my urination, like pain or increased frequency. Everything seems normal there, thankfully.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any changes in your skin, like rashes or discoloration?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t noticed any changes in my skin, like rashes or discoloration. It’s just been the cough and fever, you know? Nothing else really.

doctor_turn: How do your fibromyalgia and schizophrenia affect you on a daily basis?
patient_turn: Well, um, with fibromyalgia, I deal with a lot of achiness and fatigue, which can be pretty exhausting, you know? And for my schizophrenia, I’ve been stable since my hospitalization two years ago, but I still have to manage it with medication, like quetiapine. It’s just a lot to juggle sometimes, honestly.

doctor_turn: What medications are you currently taking for your fibromyalgia and schizophrenia?
patient_turn: I’m taking quetiapine for my schizophrenia, and I occasionally use Advil for the joint pain from fibromyalgia. I don’t have any medication allergies, so that’s good, I guess.

doctor_turn: Do you have any allergies to medications?
patient_turn: No, I don’t have any medication allergies. I’m, um, pretty lucky in that regard, I think.

doctor_turn: When was your last psychiatric hospitalization?
patient_turn: My last psychiatric hospitalization was about two years ago. Since then, I’ve been stable, which is a relief, honestly.

doctor_turn: How has your mental health been since your last hospitalization?
patient_turn: Um, it’s been stable since my last hospitalization, thankfully. I’ve been managing things pretty well, but, you know, it can still be a bit challenging at times.

doctor_turn: Can you tell me about your living situation and who you live with?
patient_turn: I live with my husband, and, um, he was recently ill with a sore throat but he recovered without any intervention. It’s just the two of us, and I don’t currently work, so I’m mostly at home.

doctor_turn: What illness did your husband have recently?
patient_turn: He had a sore throat, but he recovered without any intervention, so that’s good. I’m just glad he’s feeling better now.

doctor_turn: How long have you been smoking?
patient_turn: I’ve been smoking for, um, about 30 years now, like 2-3 cigarettes a day. I’m trying to reduce it, but it’s been tough, you know?

doctor_turn: Are you currently trying to quit smoking or reduce the amount you smoke?
patient_turn: Yeah, I’m trying to reduce the amount I smoke. It’s been a bit challenging, honestly, but I really want to cut back.

doctor_turn: Are you currently working, or are you not working at the moment?
patient_turn: I’m not currently working. I’ve just been at home, dealing with my health issues and, um, managing everything, you know?

doctor_turn: How often do you drink alcohol?
patient_turn: I rarely drink alcohol, to be honest. I haven’t had any in the past year, so it’s not really a part of my routine.

doctor_turn: Is there any family history of heart or lung disease?
patient_turn: No, there’s no family history of cardiac or pulmonary disease that I know of. So, um, that’s one less thing to worry about, I guess.

doctor_turn: Are your immunizations current and up to date?
patient_turn: Yes, my immunizations are reportedly up to date. I’ve made sure to keep everything current, you know?

doctor_turn: Have you traveled recently or been in contact with anyone who was sick?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t traveled recently, and I don’t know of any specific exposures. My husband was sick with a sore throat, but he’s better now, so I’m not sure if that’s related.

doctor_turn: When did you have your COVID-19 test done?
patient_turn: I had my COVID-19 test done recently, um, because of the symptoms I was having. I’m not exactly sure when, but it was, you know, right after I started feeling unwell.
